title: Career Pivot — Leaving Finance for Software
problem: I spent 4 years in finance before switching to software engineering. The decision took 18 months of deliberation but I never wrote any of it down. Now I can't reconstruct what tipped the balance, what alternatives I seriously considered, or what I was afraid of getting wrong. The reasoning is gone.

lineage.spawnReason: Leaving finance for software was the defining life decision that made the LAC project possible — it deserves its own provenance record.

analysis: Finance gave me precision and pattern recognition under uncertainty — both transfer directly to software engineering. The pivot wasn't impulsive: I spent 18 months building projects on weekends, talking to engineers, and stress-testing the hypothesis that I'd rather spend my time writing systems than analysing them. The decision had a clear problem, a clear set of alternatives, and a moment where the balance tipped. None of that exists anywhere. This feature.json is the first time I've written it down.

decisions.decision: Leave a stable role rather than transition internally
decisions.rationale: Internal transitions in finance stay finance-adjacent. A clean break was the only way to fully context-switch and be taken seriously as an engineer.
alternativesConsidered: Internal data/analytics role — still finance, just more technical
Part-time bootcamp while employed — not enough immersion to actually change trajectory

decisions.rationale: Bootcamps optimise for employment speed; I wanted to understand the fundamentals. The slower path built a stronger foundation and removed the 'bootcamp grad' ceiling.
alternativesConsidered: Coding bootcamp — faster but shallower, expensive
CS degree — too long, didn't need the credential

decisions.decision: Target developer tooling as first domain
decisions.rationale: Building tools for developers meant I could use my own products, get honest feedback from peers, and build in a space where quality of thinking is visibly rewarded.
alternativesConsidered: Fintech — obvious fit but felt like staying in the same world
Frontend product work — too far from systems thinking

implementation: No code. The feature.json itself is the artifact — the first written record of a decision that was made and lived but never documented.
knownLimitations: Written retrospectively — the rationale is reconstructed, not captured in the moment. Some of the original uncertainty is lost.
One person's experience; the pattern (document the pivot) generalises but the specifics don't
successCriteria: A reader who has made or is considering a major career change sees their own deliberation reflected in the structure — and considers writing it down.
